text: Writing clean code is essential for any software developer. The most important goal is to make your code readable, not just for yourself but for others who may work with it later. Good naming conventions are a key part of this. Use descriptive names for variables and functions, such as "calculateTotalPrice" instead of "calc". Keep your functions short and focused on doing one thing well. This makes your code easier to understand, test, and reuse. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, allowing you to add new features or fix bugs without breaking existing functionality.
